steps.message:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
I'm a technical writer who just joined a documentation team, and I need to set up a clean workspace for our docs repository. We have a shared documentation directory at /home/user/docs_workspace that needs to be organized with proper permissions for different document types.

Here's what I need:

1. Create the following directory structure under /home/user/docs_workspace:
   - /home/user/docs_workspace/public (for published docs anyone can read)
   - /home/user/docs_workspace/drafts (for work-in-progress, more restricted)
   - /home/user/docs_workspace/templates (read-only reference files)
   - /home/user/docs_workspace/archive (old versions, read-only)

2. Set up permissions as follows:
   - public: readable and writable by owner, readable by group and others (755 for directory)
   - drafts: readable and writable by owner only, no access for group or others (700)
   - templates: readable by everyone, writable only by owner (755 for directory)
   - archive: readable by owner and group, no access for others (750)

3. Create these starter files with the specified permissions:
   - /home/user/docs_workspace/public/index.md with content "# Documentation Home\n\nWelcome to the docs." (file permissions 644)
   - /home/user/docs_workspace/drafts/wip-feature.md with content "# Feature Draft\n\nWork in progress." (file permissions 600)
   - /home/user/docs_workspace/templates/article-template.md with content "# Title\n\n## Overview\n\n## Details\n\n## See Also" (file permissions 444)
   - /home/user/docs_workspace/archive/v1-notes.md with content "# Version 1 Notes\n\nLegacy documentation." (file permissions 440)

4. Create a manifest file at /home/user/docs_workspace/permissions_manifest.txt that documents the final state. The format must be exactly:

```
DIRECTORY_PERMISSIONS:
/home/user/docs_workspace/public:755
/home/user/docs_workspace/drafts:700
/home/user/docs_workspace/templates:755
/home/user/docs_workspace/archive:750

FILE_PERMISSIONS:
/home/user/docs_workspace/public/index.md:644
/home/user/docs_workspace/drafts/wip-feature.md:600
/home/user/docs_workspace/templates/article-template.md:444
/home/user/docs_workspace/archive/v1-notes.md:440
```

The manifest must have exactly this format with no extra whitespace, blank lines between sections (one blank line only as shown), and permissions in octal notation. The automated test will diff this file exactly.

5. Finally, create a script at /home/user/docs_workspace/verify_permissions.sh that when run outputs "PASS" if all permissions match the manifest, or "FAIL: <path>" for the first mismatched item. The script should be executable (755).


Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
user@99e1b2b9d162:~$

Analysis: The terminal is at the home directory (~). I need to create a complete documentation workspace structure at /home/user/docs_workspace with specific directories, files, and permissions. The task requires: 1) Creating 4 subdirectories with specific permissions, 2) Creating 4 files with specific content and permissions, 3) Creating a permissions manifest file, and 4) Creating an executable verification script.
Plan: I'll execute this in steps:
1. Create the base directory /home/user/docs_workspace
2. Create all subdirectories (public, drafts, templates, archive)
3. Set proper permissions on each directory
4. Create the required files with their content
5. Set proper permissions on each file
6. Create the permissions_manifest.txt with exact formatting
7. Create the verify_permissions.sh script
8. Make the script executable
9. Verify everything is set up correctly

arguments.keystrokes: echo -e '# Documentation Home\n\nWelcome to the docs.' > public/index.md
echo -e '# Feature Draft\n\nWork in progress.' > drafts/wip-feature.md

echo -e '# Title

## Overview

## Details

## See Also' > templates/article-template.md

cat > verify_permissions.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash

# Function to get octal permissions
get_perms() {
    stat -c '%a' "$1" 2>/dev/null
}

# Check directories
if [ "$(get_perms /home/user/docs_workspace/public)" != "755" ]; then
    echo "FAIL: /home/user/docs_workspace/public"
    exit 1
fi

if [ "$(get_perms /home/user/docs_workspace/drafts)" != "700" ]; then
    echo "FAIL: /home/user/docs_workspace/drafts"
    exit 1
fi

if [ "$(get_perms /home/user/docs_workspace/templates)" != "755" ]; then
    echo "FAIL: /home/user/docs_workspace/templates"
    exit 1
fi

if [ "$(get_perms /home/user/docs_workspace/archive)" != "750" ]; then
    echo "FAIL: /home/user/docs_workspace/archive"
    exit 1
fi

# Check files
if [ "$(get_perms /home/user/docs_workspace/public/index.md)" != "644" ]; then
    echo "FAIL: /home/user/docs_workspace/public/index.md"
    exit 1
fi

if [ "$(get_perms /home/user/docs_workspace/drafts/wip-feature.md)" != "600" ]; then
    echo "FAIL: /home/user/docs_workspace/drafts/wip-feature.md"
    exit 1
fi

if [ "$(get_perms /home/user/docs_workspace/templates/article-template.md)" != "444" ]; then
    echo "FAIL: /home/user/docs_workspace/templates/article-template.md"
    exit 1
fi

if [ "$(get_perms /home/user/docs_workspace/archive/v1-notes.md)" != "440" ]; then
    echo "FAIL: /home/user/docs_workspace/archive/v1-notes.md"
    exit 1
fi

echo "PASS"
EOF

steps.message:
Analysis: Good progress! All directories and files have been created with the proper permissions. I can see from the ls -la output that the directories have the correct permissions (public: 755, drafts: 700, templates: 755, archive: 750). The verification script and manifest file are also in place. Now I need to verify the content and permissions of all files, and test the verification script to ensure everything is working correctly.
Plan: I'll verify the setup by: 1) Checking the permissions of all directories and files using stat, 2) Verifying the content of each markdown file, 3) Checking the manifest file format, 4) Running the verification script to ensure it outputs PASS. This will confirm the entire workspace is set up correctly.

steps.message:
Analysis: Perfect! All components have been successfully created and verified:

1. ✅ Directory structure created with correct permissions:
   - public: 755
   - drafts: 700
   - templates: 755
   - archive: 750

2. ✅ All files created with correct content and permissions:
   - public/index.md: 644 with correct content
   - drafts/wip-feature.md: 600 with correct content
   - templates/article-template.md: 444 with correct content
   - archive/v1-notes.md: 440 with correct content

3. ✅ permissions_manifest.txt created with exact format specified (proper sections, one blank line between them, correct paths and permissions)

4. ✅ verify_permissions.sh script created, executable (755), and outputs "PASS" with exit code 0

The documentation workspace is fully set up and functional.
Plan: Perform a final comprehensive check by listing the tree structure to provide a complete overview of the workspace setup.

steps.message:
Analysis: Excellent! The recursive listing confirms everything is properly set up:

✅ All 4 directories created with correct permissions:
  - public: drwxr-xr-x (755)
  - drafts: drwx------ (700)
  - templates: drwxr-xr-x (755)
  - archive: drwxr-x--- (750)

✅ All 4 markdown files created with correct permissions:
  - public/index.md: -rw-r--r-- (644)
  - drafts/wip-feature.md: -rw------- (600)
  - templates/article-template.md: -r--r--r-- (444)
  - archive/v1-notes.md: -r--r----- (440)

✅ permissions_manifest.txt: -rw-r--r-- (644) with exact format required
✅ verify_permissions.sh: -rwxr-xr-x (755) executable and outputs "PASS"

All file contents have been verified to match specifications. The verification script successfully validates all permissions. The workspace is complete and ready for use.
Plan: The task is complete. All requirements have been met:
1. Directory structure created with proper permissions
2. All files created with correct content and permissions
3. Manifest file created with exact formatting
4. Verification script created, executable, and working correctly
